text.title: Tool Description as Prompt Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for tool descriptions treated as zero-shot teaching prompts. Each rule lifts tool-selection accuracy without a model change.

rule.statement: Every tool description MUST contain five named parts: (1) one-sentence "use this when", (2) one-sentence "do NOT use this when", (3) input contract (paths, units, formats), (4) output contract (shape, limits, sort order), (5) side effects (MUTATING / idempotent / dry-run available).

rationale.source: Anthropic engineering — SWE-bench tool-writing notes; Anthropic tool-use docs

rule.rationale: Anthropic reported state-of-the-art on SWE-bench achieved through description rewrites, not a model change. The structured form gives the model a deterministic checklist for selection and argument-fill.

rule.statement: Each description MUST stay under 200 tokens. Marketing prose ("powerful", "AI-driven", "enterprise-grade"), implementation details ("uses Bedrock under the hood"), and re-statements of the JSON schema are forbidden.

rationale.source: Anthropic tool-use docs; internal context-cost audits

rule.rationale: Longer descriptions lose the model's attention and consume the prompt budget across every turn without adding discrimination signal.

rule.statement: When two tools have overlapping use cases, each description MUST point to the other by name in its "do NOT use this when" line (mutual anti-triggers).

rationale.source: Internal — read_file vs grep_repo postmortem; faion-cli generate_implementation vs refactor_code

rule.rationale: Mutual anti-triggers collapse mis-selection between near-synonyms to near-zero in production traces.

rule.statement: Side-effect / destructive tools MUST start the description with the all-caps token `MUTATING:` (or `IRREVERSIBLE:` for non-recoverable ops) and state the safe sequencing partner (e.g. "pair with dry_run_query first").
rule.rationale: The marker is a deterministic signal for safety hooks AND for the model itself; idempotent vs mutating must not be inferred from the verb alone.

rule.statement: Tools with latency > 5s MUST declare `LATENCY:` band and explicit "do NOT poll" instruction (e.g. emit via webhook). Paginated tools MUST declare per-page count + max-page cap and the narrow-query alternative.

rationale.source: Internal — batch_train_model polling loop; search_docs enumeration loops

rule.rationale: Without caps, agents spin or brute-force-paginate; explicit caps redirect to better queries.

rule.statement: The description MUST be consistent with the JSON input_schema. Mentioned arguments must exist in the schema; schema arguments not load-bearing for the agent's decision must not be re-described (avoid duplication).
rule.rationale: Drift between description and schema is the most common cause of argument-fill errors in long-running agents.
